attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway rider who targets early moves to gain GC time via satellite positioning rather than late attacks. Shows willingness to attack on varied terrain including cobbled climbs, using breaks to exploit disorganized race situations.
team_tactics: Used as satellite GC rider in Grand Tours, sent in breakaways to gain time when race leader defense is weak. Team (INEOS/Jayco) positions him to exploit organizational chaos rather than pure climbing strength. Benefits from domestique support but not primary team focus.
terrain_preference: Excels in breakaway scenarios and cobbled/punchy climbs where positioning matters more than sustained climbing power. Struggles on steep, sustained climbs like Poggio Morella. Better suited to chaotic stages than pure mountain tests.
vulnerability: Exposed on prolonged steep climbs where pure climbing watts matter. Wet technical conditions problematic (TTT crash). Lacks finishing experience against veteran breakaway specialists. Vulnerable when race pace is controlled and breaks monitored.
key_patterns: Breakaway-to-GC conversion specialist: gained 250+ seconds through early moves multiple times (4 observations)
Survives longer than expected in mountains but loses time to pure climbers consistently (3 observations)
Attacks early on climbs to fracture groups rather than late accelerations (2 observations)
Demonstrates tenacity in breakaway survival situations across multiple seasons (3 observations)
Benefits from weak/disorganized GC race situations more than creating own opportunities (3 observations)
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 showed continued breakaway effectiveness and climbing resilience at Giro, but Vuelta TTT struggles suggest inconsistency. Still executing same satellite tactics without evolution to pure climber.
built_from: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 1
